data.summary_suggestion: Update subscriber contact information and preferences
data.description_suggestion: Updates an existing subscriber's contact information and notification preferences on a status page. Requires the status page ID and subscriber ID. Allows updating the subscriber's address (email or SMS number with country code) and granular notification settings (comma-separated component_container combinations to receive updates for). Returns the updated subscriber object. Only provided fields are modified; omitted fields retain their current values.

data.current_description:
+ statuspage_id (string, required) - Status page ID

+ all_infrastructure_affected (string, required) - Apply to all components and containers. Set to `1` to affect all infrastructure, `0` to use specific infrastructure_affected list (default: `0`).

+ infrastructure_affected (array, required) - Array of component-container ID pairs in the format `component_id-container_id`

+ incident_name (string, required) - Descriptive title for the incident

+ incident_details (string, required) - Message describing this incident

+ current_status (number, required) - The status of the components and containers affected by this incident. [View status level codes](http://kb.status.io/developers/status-codes)

+ current_state (number, required) - The state of this incident. [View state level codes](http://kb.status.io/developers/status-codes)

+ social (string, required) - Automatically post this update to Twitter/X. Set to `1` to post a message, `0` to skip.

+ notify_email (string, optional) - Notify email subscribers. Set to `1` to send notification, `0` to skip.

+ notify_sms (string, optional) - Notify SMS subscribers. Set to `1` to send notification, `0` to skip.

+ notify_webhook (string, optional) - Notify webhook subscribers. Set to `1` to send notification, `0` to skip.

+ irc (string, optional) - Notify IRC channel. Set to `1` to send notification, `0` to skip.

+ msteams (string, optional) - Notify Microsoft Teams channels. Set to `1` to send notification, `0` to skip.

+ slack (string, optional) - Notify Slack channel. Set to `1` to send notification, `0` to skip.

+ message_subject (string, optional) - The message subject for email notifications. [How to use variables in the message subject](http://kb.status.io/notifications/message-subject-variables/)
data.summary_suggestion: Create and publish a new incident
data.description_suggestion: Creates and publishes a new incident on the status page. Specify affected components and containers (or apply to all infrastructure), provide an incident name and description, and set the current status and state. The incident is immediately visible to status page viewers. Optionally configure notifications across multiple channels (email, SMS, webhook, IRC, Microsoft Teams, Slack) and automatically post to social media (Twitter/X). Returns the created incident object with its ID and details.
data.reasoning: Original summary is a path fragment without action clarity. + slack (string, optional) - Notify Slack channel. Set to `1` to send notification, `0` to skip.

+ message_subject (string, optional) - The message subject for email notifications. [How to use variables in the message subject](http://kb.status.io/notifications/message-subject-variables/)
data.summary_suggestion: Post an update to ongoing maintenance
data.description_suggestion: Posts an update message to an active maintenance event and notifies subscribers. Requires the maintenance ID and update message content. Sends notifications to subscribers via email, SMS, webhook, IRC, Microsoft Teams, and Slack based on provided flags. Optionally posts update to Twitter/X. Returns confirmation of update posting and notification delivery status.

Description is parameter-heavy but lacks clarity about the operation's effect (adding an update message to an active maintenance event), distinguishing it from 'start', and what is returned. AI agents need to understand this posts progress updates during ongoing maintenance, not state transitions.

data.current_description:
+ statuspage_id (string, required) - Status page ID

+ metric_id (string, required) - Metric ID

+ day_avg (string, required) - Average value for past 24 hours

+ day_start (string, required) - UNIX timestamp for start of metric timeframe

+ day_dates (array, required) - An array of timestamps for the past 24 hours (2014-03-28T05:43:00+00:00)

+ day_values (array, required) - An array of values matching the timestamps. Must contain exactly 24 values.

+ week_avg (string, required) - Average value for past 7 days

+ week_start (string, required) - UNIX timestamp for start of metric timeframe

+ week_dates (array, required) - An array of timestamps for the past 7 days (2014-03-28T05:43:00+00:00)

+ week_values (array, required) - An array of values matching the timestamps. Must contain exactly 7 values.

+ month_avg (string, required) - Average value for past 30 days

+ month_start (string, required) - UNIX timestamp for start of metric timeframe

+ month_dates (array, required) - An array of timestamps for the past 30 days (2014-03-28T05:43:00+00:00)

+ month_values (array, required) - An array of values matching the timestamps. Must contain exactly 30 values.
data.summary_suggestion: Update custom metric data with historical values
data.description_suggestion: Updates a custom metric with historical performance data across three time periods: 24-hour, 7-day, and 30-day windows. Each period requires an average value, start timestamp, and arrays of timestamps and corresponding values. Day values must contain exactly 24 entries, week values 7 entries, and month values 30 entries. Returns confirmation of the metric update. This operation is used to populate metric charts on the status page with historical data.

data.current_description:
+ statuspage_id (string, required) - Status page ID

+ incident_id (string, required) - Incident ID

+ incident_details (string, required) - Message describing this incident

+ social (string, required) - Automatically post this update to Twitter/X. Set to `1` to post a message, `0` to skip.

+ notify_email (string, optional) - Notify email subscribers. Set to `1` to send notification, `0` to skip.

+ notify_sms (string, optional) - Notify SMS subscribers. Set to `1` to send notification, `0` to skip.

+ notify_webhook (string, optional) - Notify webhook subscribers. Set to `1` to send notification, `0` to skip.

+ irc (string, optional) - Notify IRC channel. Set to `1` to send notification, `0` to skip.

+ msteams (string, optional) - Notify Microsoft Teams channels. Set to `1` to send notification, `0` to skip.

+ slack (string, optional) - Notify Slack channel. Set to `1` to send notification, `0` to skip.

+ message_subject (string, optional) - The message subject for email notifications. [How to use variables in the message subject](http://kb.status.io/notifications/message-subject-variables/)
data.summary_suggestion: Mark incident as resolved and notify subscribers
data.description_suggestion: Marks an incident as resolved on the specified status page and sends resolution notifications to subscribers through configured channels. Requires incident ID and resolution message. Supports selective notification delivery via email, SMS, webhook, IRC, Microsoft Teams, and Slack. Optionally posts resolution update to Twitter/X. Returns confirmation of incident resolution and notification delivery status.

+ slack (string, optional) - Notify Slack channel. Set to `1` to send notification, `0` to skip.

+ message_subject (string, optional) - The message subject for email notifications. [How to use variables in the message subject](http://kb.status.io/notifications/message-subject-variables/)
data.summary_suggestion: Post an update to ongoing maintenance
data.description_suggestion: Posts an update message to an active maintenance event and notifies subscribers. Requires the maintenance ID and update message content. Sends notifications to subscribers via email, SMS, webhook, IRC, Microsoft Teams, and Slack based on provided flags. Optionally posts update to Twitter/X. Returns confirmation of update posting and notification delivery status.
data.reasoning: Original summary is just a path fragment. Description is parameter-heavy but lacks clarity about the operation's effect (adding an update message to an active maintenance event), distinguishing it from 'start', and what is returned. AI agents need to understand this posts progress updates during ongoing maintenance, not state transitions.
